Security notes and recommendation
- The first email (support@techservices.com) appears to be the official TechServices support address and is currently unread in your inbox.
- The second email comes from an external AOL address and includes an unrelated-looking domain (password-reset-12bh12.com); that one looks suspicious/phishy.
- Best practice: avoid clicking links in emails you didn’t expect. Instead, go directly to the official TechServices website (type the URL yourself) and initiate a password reset from there, or use the link from the official support@techservices.com message if you are certain it’s legitimate.
- If you want, I can:
  - Mark the authentic-looking message as important, move it to a folder, or forward it to IT/security for verification.
  - Open the unread TechServices message and confirm any additional details.
  - Start a password reset for you by creating an event or sending an email to support (if you authorize me).

What would you like me to do next?
